jobs.description:

• Author post-mortem analyses to determine areas for optimization and improvement on past content investment decisions
• Set content category level performance and efficiency goals and report to PV leadership on actual results, and dive deep to deliver insights into drivers of variance – inclusive of Prime Video content factors and wider Prime dynamics.
• Provide timely, accurate and complete, financial forecasting and reporting for our US Prime Video service and content budgets, including close partnership with Prime Video Advertising.


Key job responsibilities
- 5+ years of finance experience or a related analytical field.
- BA/BS Degree in Finance, Economics, Business, Engineering or similar discipline with analytical bias;
- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
- Strong resourcefulness and ability to find or develop compelling data given limited resources and time;
- Demonstrated expertise with building financial models and processes using MS Excel;
- Demonstrated ability to meet deadlines while managing multiple projects with cross-functional teams
- Experience working in an ambiguous environment;
- An eye for continuous process improvement, including the ability to innovate and simplify finance and operational processes.

jobs.title: Finance Manager, Prime Video Finance

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 3+ years of finance or a related analytical field experience
- 2+ years of building financial and operational reports/data sets that inform business decision-making experience
- Bachelor's degree
jobs.description:
Elevate Entertainment Finance: Shape the Future of Streaming Content

We're seeking a strategic finance professional to drive financial innovation in the world of original series production. Your analytical expertise will directly influence the creative decisions that bring ground-breaking entertainment to millions of global viewers.

Key job responsibilities
Build and maintain databases and automated workflows to support Original Series forecasting, reporting, and analysis.
Dive deep into data sources to identify discrepancies, determine root causes, and implement solutions that ensure reporting accuracy and financial compliance.
Collaborate with production, production finance, accounting, business affairs, music, and consumer products teams to model financial scenarios and support data-driven decision making.
Develop and execute projects that streamline financial processes, enhance controllership, and improve forecast accuracy with measurable results.
Proactively analyze financial data to identify trends, propose improvements, and provide recommendations that influence business performance across Original Series finance.

A day in the life
Your day begins with reviewing financial data across multiple systems to ensure accuracy in financial reporting and analysis. You'll collaborate with cross-functional partners in production, business affairs, and accounting to gather information needed for building financial models. Throughout the day, you'll work on automating workflows, creating database solutions, and developing reports that provide transparency into key metrics. You may be asked to run a music royalty forecast or profit waterfall analysis to support deal negotiations.

About the team
Our team oversees end-to-end Original Series finance, from development through to post-release reporting and long-term forecasts. We work closely with our partners in creative, production, and accounting to track and manage team performance against key metrics and annual budgets. We provide financial insights that shape content strategy and investment decisions, ensuring resources are allocated effectively across the portfolio.

As financial stewards, we balance creative ambition with fiscal responsibility, developing systems and processes that scale with our growing content slate. We're committed to providing timely, accurate financial reporting and analysis that enables informed business decisions at every stage of the content lifecycle.

jobs.title: Sr. Financial Analyst, Series, Amazon Original Series

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 2+ years of applying key financial performance indicators (KPIs) to analyses experience
- Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, Economics or a highly analytical field (e.g., Engineering, Math, and Computer Science)
- Knowledge of standard software including Excel, Access, Oracle, Essbase, SQL and VBA skills
- 2+ years of finance experience
- Experience using data to influence business decisions
jobs.description:
The Amazon Advertising Deals Desk team is seeking an experienced and highly motivated Financial Analyst (FA) to join the Deal Desk team and support core aspects of the Direct Advertiser Agreements program. This team owns benchmarking, deal evaluation, defining guardrails, negotiating deal terms and support monetization initiatives. As a FA you will have the exposure to all aspects of deal making and support the deliverables for this team.

This role will work closely with Sales and Product teams to evaluate and determine the long-term strategy to drive incremental Advertising Revenue. Day to day activities include reviewing program adoption, evaluating and building long term revenue proposals, support accounting, and reporting initiatives such as monthly and quarterly business reviews. As a Financial Analyst, you will be at the center of our deal approval operations, reviewing campaigns and deals daily, ensuring compliance with pricing guidelines, and managing the approval workflow for both Managed Services and Sales-enabled self-service execution deals. You will work extensively within our Order Management System (OMS) to evaluate applied rates against Pricing Hub benchmarks and make critical approval decisions that impact revenue and advertiser relationships.

This role requires an individual with excellent analytical abilities, business acumen and strong interpersonal skills to partner with Sales and Product teams. The successful candidate will be a self-starter, comfortable with ambiguity, with strong attention to detail, and an ability to work in a fast-paced and ever-changing environment.

Key job responsibilities
a) Manage Deal review and approvals
b) Support Wider Deals Desk team in strengthening the reporting function
c) Partner with the product teams to streamline the existing workflows
jobs.description_short: The Amazon Advertising Deals Desk team is seeking an experienced and highly motivated Financial Analyst (FA) to join the Deal Desk team and support core aspects of the Direct Advertiser Agreements program.

jobs.title: Deals Desk Analyst, Advertising Deals Desk

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 12+ years of tax, finance or a related analytical field experience
- Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, Economics or a highly analytical field (e.g., Engineering, Math, and Computer Science)
- 7+ years of finance technology, forecast & budgeting, or other finance-focused competency experience
- Experience with Tableau, Python, SQB, Quicksight, Cognos or or other BI packages
jobs.description:
We are seeking a Senior Finance Manager for a strategic finance role with dual responsibility. First, it will support IMDb - world's most popular source for movie, TV, and celebrity content, attracting over 250 million unique visitors monthly - on all operational and financial strategy across multiple revenue streams. Second, this role leads FP&A function for Amazon Publisher Monetization team, which drives a muti-billion ads business across key Amazon publishers, and manages rhythm of the business them.

This opportunity is ideal for a finance professional passionate about entertainment and innovation, ready to drive impact in a dynamic, fast-paced environment. This critical role demands a self-starter with exceptional business modeling capabilities and meticulous attention to detail.

Key job responsibilities
Financial Planning & Reporting
• Partner with IMDb leaders to architect financial plans and analyses across advertising, IMDbPro subscriptions, and data licensing revenue streams; own OP planning, MBRs, QBRs, headcount planning, and executive reporting
• Build and maintain comprehensive financial models spanning the entire IMDb P&L—OpEx, COGS, and revenue
• Collaborate with Amazon Publisher Monetization finance teams to consolidate financials and execute MFRs, QFRs, goals meetings, and headcount tracking

Strategic Financial Analysis
• Evaluate business cases and financial returns for strategic investments, developing frameworks to measure ROI and ensure alignment with organizational priorities
• Lead the annual Goals mechanism to drive measurable performance outcomes in a continuously evolving business landscape
• Champion process automation and innovation within the finance function, leveraging AI tools and advanced analytics to enhance forecasting accuracy and eliminate manual reporting cycles

A day in the life
If you enjoy being "CFO" of a strategic business, building scalable processes and reporting then this will be a good match.

About the team
APM team works to maximize ads revenue for all strategic Amazon businesses, including IMDb. The APM Finance team supports them achieve their charter through insightful analysis, forward looking planning, budgeting and variance analysis.

jobs.description:
The Amazon Advertising FP&A team is seeking an experienced and highly motivated Senior Financial Analyst to join the team. The role will help establish and monitor OpEx, Goals, and broader leadership reporting (MFR). The SFA will partner closely with Data Engineers and Business Intelligence Engineers to build tools (with AI functionality) that will create enhanced visibility in these areas. This role will work closely with Sales teams, regional Finance PoCs, and Ads Central FP&A.

This role requires an individual with excellent analytical abilities, high business acumen and strong interpersonal skills to partner with Finance Sales and Product teams. The successful candidate will be a self-starter, comfortable with ambiguity, with strong attention to detail, and an ability to work in a fast-paced and ever-changing environment.

Key job responsibilities
Primary responsibilities include:
- Establish new metrics and validation
- Lead reporting and deep dives
- Maintain and develop standard reporting cadence and metrics for leadership benchmarking
- Contribute with deep-dive analyses and insights in Monthly and Quarterly Business Reviews


A day in the life
This role will help drive standardization of reporting and insights for a global ad sales finance team.

About the team
The Ad Sales Finance team supports the global ad sales organization around the world.

jobs.description:
Over $500 billion is spent each year on digital advertising worldwide, all while the industry experiences foundational changes in terms of media advertisers want to buy, how potential customers spend time online, and an ever evolving privacy landscape. Amazon Advertising Finance is looking for a Finance Manager to join one of the fastest growing divisions within Amazon and support our Ad Tech team on building our Streaming TV and Audio business and help our advertisers reach customers and drive outcomes. This role will work hand in hand with the product team, sales team, and other finance members to evaluate new partnerships, size the potential opportunity of new product launches, set goals/targets, analyze petabytes worth of data, make suggestions based on analysis, and write strategy documents.

Key job responsibilities
Partner closely with the business on strategic initiatives and to size the opportunity (customers, ad spend, etc.) of new product launches
Analyze rich supply, demand, and auction/bidding, data in order to provide tangible insights that influence real-time decision making
Establish new performance metrics to track and communicate business health and drive insightful deep dives into our business
Write 1-6 page strategy documents
Participate in and lead parts of the quarterly guidance and yearly operational plans, including revenue forecasting

jobs.description:
Amazon Leo is Amazon's low Earth orbit satellite network. Our mission is to deliver fast, reliable internet connectivity to customers beyond the reach of existing networks. From individual households to schools, hospitals, businesses, and government agencies, Amazon Leo will serve people and organizations operating in locations without reliable connectivity.

Export Control Requirement: Due to applicable export control laws and regulations, candidates must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (i.e., current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.

As a member of the Amazon Leo's Materials team, you will play a crucial role in developing advanced materials and processes that enable the cost-effective and reliable production of our satellites. You will be responsible for conducting hands-on laboratory testing and failure analysis of flight hardware and raw stock materials, as well as developing next generation materials to improve satellite performance. You will work closely with external vendors to establish process control and identify new opportunities for product development and improvement.

jobs.description:
Join Amazon MGM Studios as we expand our theatrical distribution globally. We're building a best-in-class finance organization from the ground up to support international theatrical operations. This is an opportunity to shape the financial foundation of a new operation while working with content that reaches millions of viewers worldwide.

As a Senior Financial Analyst, you'll be part of Amazon MGM's theatrical expansion, consolidating data from all international teams and partnering with distribution and marketing teams to drive strategic financial decisions that support our films in international markets.


Key job responsibilities
• Build and maintain sophisticated financial models for budgeting, forecasting, and scenario planning across multiple film releases
• Own end-to-end financial reporting consolidation across all territories, delivering real-time business performance insights that drive decision-making
• Develop ROI models and investment analysis frameworks to evaluate marketing campaign effectiveness
• Work closely with global colleagues to aggregate local market insights and create comprehensive financial forecasts and models across global territories
• Develop and implement scalable financial processes for international theatrical operations
• Work closely with global colleagues to aggregate local market insights and create comprehensive financial forecasts and models across global territories


A day in the life
Imagine diving into a world where financial analysis meets creative storytelling. You'll navigate complex international financial landscapes, translating data into strategic recommendations that power global entertainment initiatives. Your work will directly impact how theatrical campaigns are planned, executed, and optimized across diverse markets.

About the team
Our global finance team is a collaborative network of strategic thinkers who are passionate about supporting creative storytelling through rigorous financial insights. We work closely with marketing, regional leaders, and creative teams to ensure financial strategies align with broader business objectives and artistic vision.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 8+ years of building financial and operational reports/data sets that inform business decision-making experience
- 8+ years of applying key financial performance indicators (KPIs) to analyses experience

jobs.description:
Amazon MGM Studios (AMS) Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) team is looking for a Finance Manager to support the business and finance teams across theatrical film, third party scripted & unscripted TV, MGM+, consumer products and global distribution, with a focus on managing the financial planning efforts and direct ownership of the Profit & Loss consolidation. This role will drive the core financial planning cycles, including Month-End Close, Quarterly Guidance forecasts and Annual Operating Plans, as well as ongoing controllership. Further, this role will lead FP&A efforts around special projects, such as M&A and business/system integrations. This role provides exposure to both finance partners and business operations teams.

Key job responsibilities
The successful candidate will have strong leadership skills, solid business judgement capable of driving an organization to the right results, strong business partnership building, exceptional excel/systems skills, keen attention to detail, and strong verbal and written communication skills. They will have demonstrated success in a fast-paced, complex, global, results-oriented environment. The team needs a self-starter, who is able to work efficiently and autonomously. They must be able to think and act both strategically and tactically and manage multiple competing priorities effectively.

• Lead month end, quarterly, annual operating financial planning working cross functionally with various teams, including Prime Video finance, accounting, and business operations
• Ensure data is accurately reflected in financial planning systems, including Essbase and Cognos
• Drive process automation to streamline current processes and enhance financial controllership
• Ad hoc project support on company-wide strategic initiatives
• Analyze complex financial data and provide accurate and timely reports to key stakeholders, highlighting key insights and recommendations
• Support the month-end close process and ensure accurate and timely financial reporting
• Assist in developing and implementing financial policies, procedures, and internal controls

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 4+ years of working in mechanical engineering or equivalent experience
- Experience with prototyping and implementation
- 2+ years of post PhD experience experience
- PhD, or Master's degree in physics, applied physics, electrical engineering, or an equivalent degree in a similar field
- 4+ years cleanroom experience in the thermal evaporation of superconducting metal and other materials
- Experience working on vacuum hardware systems used for thin film deposition
- Experience in thin films characterization (e.g. AFM, SEM, XPS, XRD, EDX, profilometry, reflectometry, and ellipsometry)
jobs.description:
The Center for Quantum Computing (CQC) is seeking a superconducting thin film engineer. The CQC is a multi-disciplinary team of scientists, engineers, and technicians, on a mission to develop a fault-tolerant quantum computer. We are looking to hire a hardware development engineer with superconducting thin film deposition and complementary fabrication experience. The engineer will be focused on thin films deposition to support fabrication and studies of Josephson Junction elements, superconducting resonators, 3D integration components (like superconducting bumps) and other critical elements of superconducting circuits. The position is on-site at our lab, located on the in Pasadena, CA. The ideal candidate will have had prior experience depositing superconducting films and studying blanket film and fabricated device performance. We are looking for candidates with strong engineering principles, resourcefulness and a data driven approach. Organization and communication skills are essential.

Diverse Experiences
Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship and Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Key job responsibilities
* Own thermal evaporation processes used for superconducting metals
* Deep dive into the hardware, process and physics related to Josephson Junctions or metal-insulator-metal fabrication
* Hands-on production support of deposition processes used to fabricate superconducting circuit elements such as indium bumps, airbridges, resonators etc
* Lead process control of deposition processes
* Devise experiments and provide recommendations for improvement of fabrication processes.
* Communicate findings with colleagues by way of crisp documentation and presentations.

A day in the life
The role will be vital to the fabrication team and quantum computing device integration mechanism. The candidate will provide drive improvement of the superconducting thin film deposition area that involve thermal deposition, physical vapor deposition and chemical vapor deposition processes. The candidate will work with other scientists and engineers in fabricating superconducting devices. This role is flexible but is mainly 2nd shift. The position may require occasional weekend work.

About the team
Our team is comprised of scientists and engineers who are building hardware that enables quantum computing technologies. Doing that requires the fabrication of quantum devices, which necessitates staying close to the details and analyzing data while building tools to better understand the data.
